query: Cursor for iOS complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Cursor is rated "Bad" with 1.7 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
It’s incredibly frustrating to give a company your money, having used the highest usage tier previously as well, only to have Stephen treat you like an automated nuisance over a basic email verification gap. The tool itself is good, and I'll definitely be burning through the rest of the compute power I paid for until this cycle ends, but I’ve already set it to cancel. I'm moving my projects over to Windsurf and Continue.dev. Save yourself the headache if you ever run into an account issue with their team

June 15, 2026

Unprompted review

Useful 1

Share

SM

Shomsky Michael US•3 reviews

Jun 22, 2026

Image 36: Rated 5 out of 5 stars [...] Looking at 190 reviews, most reviewers were let down by their experience overall. Many customers found the user experience poor, with the interface frequently changing and becoming inefficient, making the tool difficult to use and often leading to errors. The subscription and pricing models were a major source of dissatisfaction, with many feeling that costs were too high and billing practices wer e unclear or manipulative. Reviewers also reported extremely negative experiences with customer service, citing unresponsiveness and a lack of support for critical issues. The artificial intelligence features, while initially promising, often failed to provide accurate or coherent responses, creating more work rather than enhancing productivity. However, some people also felt that the AI tools, [...] Jun 15, 2026

Image 45: Rated 3 out of 5 stars

## Useful and powerful, but frustrating, confusing, and credit-heavy
Cursor has been genuinely useful, but also much more frustrating and time-consuming than I expected.

I used it to rebuild one of my websites, and I will give credit where it is due: the final direction of the site is much better than what I had before. It helped bring in new ideas, improved concepts, and some much-needed structure that made the website feel more modern and useful. For that alone, I do see the value in the tool.

That said, getting to that point took far more effort than I expected.

results.title: I Built an IOS App in 3 Days with Literally No Prior Swift Knowledge
results.content:
Cursor fixed it on the next try: The problem is that the onTapGesture is applied to the Picker, but in SwiftUI, segmented pickers don’t always respond well to tap gestures because they have their own internal gesture handling. The onChange modifier only triggers when the selection actually changes, not when the same value is tapped.

Another thing worth mentioning is the “context used” percentage in Cursor. It automatically captures relevant context from your codebase, so you don’t have to tell it which files to look at. But your chat history is also included in that context, and sometimes it contains old problems or irrelevant code.

That extra clutter can increase token usage or make Cursor less efficient. When the context used percentage increases, I clear the chat history. [...] I described the issue to Cursor, and it explained both what was causing the problem and how to fix it. Here are two examples of how Cursor can help you learn along the way:

The issue is that rewardQuote is empty on the first tap because handleRewardTap() sets it, but the sheet is presented immediately. I need to ensure the quote is set before showing the sheet.

Fixed the issue! The problem was that showReward = true was being set immediately after rewardQuote, but SwiftUI’s sheet presentation can happen before the state update is fully processed.

## Use Cursor more efficiently

Cursor is a great tool but there are some ways to make it more efficient. [...] Let’s assume code fails or it doesn’t execute correctly. If you know the problematic part, highlight that section and add to the chat. It definitely makes it easier for Cursor to fix problem.

In Brush Tracker, I was having a problem when setting up notifications. I defined the problem and asked Cursor to fix it. Cursor told me it was fixed but the problem still existed. Then, from the code changes I found where the problem was occurring and added that part to the chat.

results.title: Cursor - App Store - Apple
results.content: Cursor is the agent-native way to build ambitious software. Kick off as many agents as you want in parallel, review changes, and ship PRs, right from your phone. Introducing Cursor for iOS: the best way to build, review, and ship software from anywhere. Kick off and manage agents to implement changes, fix bugs, and keep projects moving forward while you're away from your desk. BUILD FROM ANYWHERE With Cursor in your pocket, you can start new work the moment inspiration strikes. Launch agents directly from your phone to implement features, investigate issues, or answer questions about your codebase. Track progress across long-running agents and review completed work no matter where you are. Cursor helps you think through complex problems with a deep understanding of your codebase. VERIFY [...] problems with a deep understanding of your codebase. VERIFY WORK WITH DEMOS Agents can test changes in their own sandbox and iterate until they've validated their output. Cursor can produce artifacts like videos, screenshots, and logs, so you can quickly verify its work before moving to the next step. By giving agents their own virtual machines with full development environments, they are able to work more autonomously to create merge-ready PRs. POINT AND DRAW TO FIX Take a screenshot, circle an issue, and drop comments to give agents visual feedback directly on images. Cursor understands annotations and uses them as context to make precise updates. Visual inputs help shrink the distance between what you see and what the agent understands. This allows you to iterate faster when refining [...] Cursor is designed to help individuals and teams build ambitious software. Always use the best model for the task at hand with access to any frontier model from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and more. Cursor's own agentic model, Composer, delivers frontier-level intelligence while optimizing for speed and token-efficiency. With Cursor for iOS, you can: • Launch coding agents from anywhere • Track and manage active engineering work • Review screenshots and videos of changes • Annotate images to provide visual feedback • Inspect diffs and merge pull requests • Talk to agents with voice conversations • Understand unfamiliar codebases faster Whether you’re maintaining production systems or building your next side project, Cursor helps you turn your ideas into working software from anywhere. ———

results.title: iOS 26 bugs still persist on 26.1 - Support - Discourse Meta
results.content:
# iOS 26 bugs still persist on 26.1

I was asked to make a new topic for this because the main announcement wasn’t the right place to discuss it.

The iOS 26 bug where the cursor position goes wrong was improved in 26.1 but not completely fixed. I can still replicate it in many ways, on my own Discourse website and on here.

Here is a video of me on my site:

You can still break it by minimising the post edit screen and then maximising it again.

It also can happen when switching apps and coming back. I switched to the Settings app to get a screenshot of my iOS version for a DM on here and when I came back the cursor was misaligned:

IMG_7572

IMG\_75721206×436 63.9 KB

IMG_7572 [...] :thinking:

cursor is far removed from where the text is

I only read this post but came here to say that. iOS 26 has been awful for my old iPhone 11 (very slow) but this specific thing started after the upgrade. It’s very frequent. The temporary solution (as before with the buttons not working) is to restart the app.

I’ve found turning the phone landscape and then back to portrait can fix it. Landscape is often better.

That rings a bell. It fixes some Discourse mobile problems for me, like when a text box shoots off the screen as soon as you touch it. I’ll try it next time the cursor parts company from its characters.

P.S. iOS 26.1.

Here’s how my landscape looks :flushed_face:

:flushed_face:

It’s mostly unusable on Safari:

IMG_4173

IMG\_41732079×960 132 KB

IMG_4173
